page: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance belonging to the Dplsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The CPU architecture is arm64, with each core hosting a single thread, providing a 1:1 mapping of vCPUs to physical cores. Cache resources include 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 128 MB of L3 cache. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, though it is premium storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance and efficient data processing capabilities. The instance achieved a top-tier rating in the PassMark memory score and demonstrated strong performance in uncached large-block memory bandwidth tests, reaching 310.02 GB/sec for read operations and 124.68 GB/sec for write operations. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also registered strong results. Conversely, compute-heavy tasks such as stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core execution, and static web serving throughput fell within the average performance tier. Large language model inference on a medium 7B model showed a weak prompt processing speed of 24.69 tokens/sec, though text generation remained average at 14.36 tokens/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the D16pls_v6 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and the complete absence of local storage. This design prioritizes compute density over memory capacity, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require dedicated physical cores but do not demand large memory footprints. The ARM-based architecture offers a favorable performance-per-watt profile, translating to qualitative cost efficiency for scale-out compute tasks. This instance is suited for application servers, containerized microservices, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and lightweight web serving workloads that can leverage external premium storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dplsv6 family, featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. Operating on the arm64 architecture, it provides 32.0 GB of system memory, representing a low-memory configuration of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage attachments. Performance benchmarks show top-tier PassMark memory scores and strong uncached large-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, standard CPU compute and static web serving benchmarks align with average tiers, and medium-model LLM prompt processing is weak. Given the 1:2 memory-to-CPU ratio and lack of local storage, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-dense, memory-light workloads. It is suited for containerized microservices, multi-threaded data decompression, and application servers utilizing external storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v6 is a 16-vCPU ARM-based virtual machine featuring a 3.4 GHz Neoverse-N2 processor, 32 GB of RAM, and strong memory bandwidth, designed for memory-light, compute-dense workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and strong memory bandwidth performance.
tagline: An ARM-powered, compute-dense virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores and high memory bandwidth for memory-constrained, scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Top-tier PassMark memory score and strong uncached memory bandwidth
No local storage or GPU, requiring external premium storage
Suited for containerized microservices and multi-threaded decompression
